title: SPIRULINA
description: Spirulina (Arthrospira platensis) is a blue-green microalgae rich in protein (60-70%), essential amino acids, vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, and bioactive compounds including phycocyanin and essential fatty acids. It functions as a nutritional supplement with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties through multiple mechanisms including enhancement of glutathione production, reduction of oxidative stress, and modulation of immune cell function. Primarily used as a whole-food nutritional supplement for general health support, immune enhancement, and athletic performance.

research.title: Potential of Spirulina platensis as a feed supplement for poultry to enhance growth performance and immune modulation
research.summary: Review demonstrating Spirulina's ability to enhance immune function and growth performance through anti-inflammatory and antioxidant mechanisms, with potential to reduce drug resistance issues in livestock.

research.title: The antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and anti-inflammatory activities of Spirulina: an overview
research.summary: Comprehensive review confirming Spirulina's antioxidant, immunomodulatory, and anti-inflammatory properties through multiple bioactive compounds including phycocyanin and phenolics.

research.title: Spirulina Microalgae and Brain Health: A Scoping Review of Experimental and Clinical Evidence
research.summary: Evidence shows Spirulina provides neuroprotective benefits through antioxidant and anti-inflammatory mechanisms, may reduce mental fatigue, and supports vascular health in brain vessels.

research.title: A Review of the Health-Promoting Properties of Spirulina with a Focus on athletes' Performance and Recovery
research.summary: Review indicating Spirulina supplementation can improve body composition, physical performance, and reduce exercise-induced oxidative stress in athletes through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

research.title: Beneficial Effects of Spirulina Consumption on Brain Health
research.summary: Spirulina demonstrates neuroprotective effects through glial cell modulation and may help prevent or slow progression of neurodegenerative diseases including Parkinson's disease.

research.title: Effects of spirulina (Arthrospira) platensis supplementation on inflammation, physical and mental quality of life in patients with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis
research.summary: Triple-blind placebo-controlled trial showing Spirulina supplementation may reduce inflammation and improve quality of life metrics in MS patients.

research.title: The True Value of Spirulina
research.summary: Analysis highlighting Spirulina's high nutrient content but also warning about potential contamination with cyanotoxins, heavy metals, pesticides, and PAHs requiring careful quality monitoring.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report positive experiences with Spirulina for maintaining healthy energy levels, immune support, and overall wellness, though quality sourcing concerns and contamination risks are frequently mentioned as important considerations.

effects.label: General Wellness:
effects.description: Most effective as a whole-food nutritional supplement providing broad-spectrum micronutrients, with users reporting cumulative benefits over months rather than immediate effects

effects.label: Quality-Dependent Results:
effects.description: Effectiveness varies significantly based on source quality, with pure, non-GMO forms from reputable sources showing better results than lower-grade products

effects.label: Complementary Benefits:
effects.description: Works best as part of a comprehensive health approach including proper diet, exercise, and sleep rather than as a standalone solution

effects.label: Individual Response:
effects.description: Effects vary by person, with some experiencing pronounced benefits within weeks while others notice subtler improvements over longer periods

dosage.label: Daily Powder:
dosage.description: 1-2 teaspoons (approximately 3-6 grams) of spirulina powder mixed in smoothies or water is the most common dosing method

dosage.label: Tablet Form:
dosage.description: 3-6 grams daily split across meals, with some users taking 1000mg tablets multiple times per day for convenience

dosage.label: Loading Phase:
dosage.description: Some users report better results starting with smaller doses (1 gram) and gradually increasing to full dose over 1-2 weeks to assess tolerance

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Most commonly taken in morning smoothies or with breakfast, though timing appears less critical than consistency of daily use

sideEffects.label: Digestive Adjustment:
sideEffects.description: Initial mild digestive changes, bloating, or green-colored stools are common in first 1-2 weeks but typically resolve with continued use

sideEffects.label: Contamination Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Users report concerns about heavy metal contamination, bird feces, and toxins in lower-quality sources, emphasizing importance of third-party testing

sideEffects.label: Taste and Palatability:
sideEffects.description: Strong earthy, seaweed-like taste is frequently mentioned as unpleasant, leading most users to mix it in smoothies rather than taking alone

sideEffects.label: Allergic Reactions:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of allergic reactions or sensitivity, particularly in those with seafood or iodine sensitivities

availability.label: Quality Brands:
availability.description: Reputable brands mentioned include quality brands, quality brands, and specialized sources like New Farmers for fresh spirulina, with emphasis on organic, non-GMO certification

availability.label: Sourcing Concerns:
availability.description: Users strongly advise avoiding spirulina sourced from China or unknown origins due to contamination risks; Hawaiian and controlled environment sources preferred

availability.label: Form Options:
availability.description: Available as powder (most economical), tablets/capsules (most convenient), and fresh/frozen (highest quality but limited availability and requires refrigeration)
